statement.p: Identify the coefficients for the quadratic equation using the standard form from Theorem1.2.1.

solution.p: Take the coefficient of x^2 for the value of a\text{,} the coefficient of x for b\text{,} and the constant for c\text{.} In this case, they are a = {2}\text{,} b = {-5}\text{,} c = {-25}\text{.}

statement.p: Using the quadratic formula, solve the equation.

solution.p: Recall that the quadratic formula is given in Theorem1.2.1.
You already identified a = {2}\text{,} b = {-5}\text{,} and c = {-25}\text{,} so the results are:

conclusion.p: This conclusion is just here for testing.
